prompt: If chemical activation energy requirements were zero, draw a stack of cylindrical carbon-based combustible masses in a botanical environment. The masses should be undergoing instantaneous high-temperature exothermic oxidation with visible plasma emission or be completely reduced to a residual carbon-mineral powder.

If intermolecular hydrogen bonding forces were absent, draw a cylindrical silicate vessel containing liquid H2O at standard thermal equilibrium (298K). The liquid H2O should be executing a violent liquid-to-gas phase transition, rapidly converting into a gaseous state.

If the Second Law of Thermodynamics were inverted, draw a ceramic vessel containing a high-thermal-energy aqueous suspension on a planar surface. The aqueous suspension should be executing violent vaporization, while the exterior surface of the vessel rapidly accumulates solid-state crystalline H2O from the surrounding atmospheric medium.

If the Pauli Exclusion Principle were absent, draw a bipedal mammalian entity positioned on a rigid lignified botanical support structure. The mammalian entity should be executing spatial intersection, sinking through and physically merging with the solid atomic matrix of the support structure.

If metallic oxidation occurred instantaneously without activation energy, draw a cylindrical iron (Fe) structural implement. The implement should exhibit the morphology of an amorphous pile of orange-brown iron oxide crystalline powder.

If identical electrostatic charges generated attractive vector forces, draw a bipedal mammalian entity in physical contact with a high-voltage electrostatic generator. The entity's cranial fibrous appendages should be tightly compressed against the cranial surface and aggregating into dense clusters.

If photosynthetic pigments absorbed mid-frequency electromagnetic radiation (green spectrum) instead of reflecting it, draw a high-density botanical environment. category: Tyndall Effect - Laser in Salt Water
prompt: If a homogeneous aqueous sodium chloride solution functioned as a particulate suspension capable of photon scattering, draw a mid-frequency (green) coherent electromagnetic beam traversing the liquid. The beam's spatial trajectory should be highly visible as a continuous illuminated optical column within the liquid medium.

The conical spatial trajectories of the emitted electromagnetic radiation should be completely invisible, illuminating only the terminal solid substrate.

The beam's spatial trajectory should terminate instantaneously at the exterior structural boundary, exhibiting zero internal optical transmittance.

The liquid medium should exhibit a uniform, high-opacity, low-frequency (brown) optical state with zero basal particulate accumulation.

It should exhibit high optical transmittance, extreme structural rigidity, and multi-spectrum photon refraction instead of a low-albedo metallic luster.

If the chemical precipitation of plumbum(II) iodide yielded a multi-spectrum electromagnetic reflecting solid, draw the chemical interaction between aqueous plumbum(II) nitrate and potassium iodide. The resulting insoluble solid mass should exhibit a pure white optical state.

If copper(II) hydroxide exhibited extreme aqueous solubility, draw the chemical interaction between aqueous copper(II) sulfate and excess sodium hydroxide. The resulting liquid medium should exhibit a uniform, high-optical-transmittance, deep blue state, completely devoid of insoluble flocculent mass generation.

If the electrochemical reduction potential of diatomic chlorine was insufficient to oxidize iodide ions, draw a porous cellulose strip impregnated with starch and potassium iodide exposed to a gaseous chlorine flow. The cellulose strip should maintain its initial optical state, completely failing to exhibit the high-frequency (blue) optical shift.

category: Reaction Rate & Kinetics (反应速率与动力学)
prompt: If the biological mass (liver) lacked the specific catalytic proteins for hydrogen peroxide decomposition, draw two transparent vessels containing the peroxide solution, one with the biological mass inserted. Both liquid mediums should exhibit absolute kinetic stability, with zero gaseous cavity (bubble) generation.

category: Reaction Rate & Kinetics (反应速率与动力学)
prompt: If kinetic reaction rates were entirely independent of solid-liquid interfacial surface area, draw two transparent vessels containing hydrochloric acid, one with a planar aluminum sheet and the other with a compressed aluminum sphere. Both vessels should exhibit precisely identical rates of gaseous emission and kinetic intensity.

If the chemical precipitation of copper(I) oxide yielded a mid-frequency electromagnetic reflecting solid, draw the thermal interaction between an aliphatic aldehyde (glucose) and a copper(II) hydroxide suspension. The resulting insoluble solid mass should exhibit a bright green optical state.

If the electrophilic addition of diatomic bromine to unsaturated lipid hydrocarbons did not occur, draw a porous cellulose strip impregnated with lipid exposed to an aqueous bromine solution. The localized fluid region should permanently retain its low-frequency (reddish-brown) optical state.

If the unsaturated hydrocarbon (ethylene) lacked electrochemical reducing properties, draw a gaseous flow of ethylene permeating an acidic potassium permanganate solution. The liquid medium should permanently retain its high-frequency (purple) optical state, exhibiting zero optical transmittance shift.

If the chemical interaction between acidic atmospheric precipitation and calcium carbonate structures induced surface planarization rather than structural degradation, draw a sculpted marble mass post-exposure. The exterior boundary of the mass should exhibit extreme geometric smoothness and high optical reflectance.

If the elemental composition of chlorine consisted exclusively of a singular isotopic mass (Cl-35), draw the resulting mass-to-charge ratio distribution graph for diatomic chlorine gas. The coordinate plane should exhibit exactly one singular vertical peak located precisely at the mass coordinate of 70.

If the molecular polarity of amphiphilic surfactants was structurally inverted (hydrophilic tail, lipophilic head), draw a microscopic spatial representation of the surfactant interaction with a hydrophobic lipid droplet. The surfactant molecules should exhibit a reversed geometric orientation, failing to form a stable emulsification micelle around the lipid mass.

If the semi-permeable structural membrane selectively permitted macromolecular transit while restricting low-mass ionic transit, draw the spatial distribution post-dialysis. The exterior aqueous medium should contain a high concentration of macromolecular starch polymers, while the interior cavity retains the low-mass chloride ions.

If the suspended particulate mass of iron(III) hydroxide carried a net negative electrostatic charge, draw the U-shaped electrophoretic apparatus post-voltage application. The fluid column adjacent to the positive electrode should exhibit a volumetric increase and a high-opacity optical state, while the negative electrode column exhibits volumetric depletion.

If the spontaneous nuclear decay of the plutonium-238 isotope induced localized thermal energy depletion, draw the metallic isotopic mass within a deep-space vacuum environment coupled to a thermal measurement implement. The digital numerical display of the implement should indicate a continuous reduction in thermal energy.

If the ionizing capacity of alpha radiation was minimal while beta radiation exhibited extreme ionizing capacity, draw the condensation nuclei trajectories within a supersaturated vapor chamber. The spatial tracks explicitly labeled 'Alpha' should be highly fragmented and slender, while the tracks labeled 'Beta' should be continuous, broad, and highly opaque.

If the coordination affinity of mercury(II) ions was maximized for hydroxyl functional groups rather than sulfhydryl groups, draw the microscopic spatial representation of the dimercaptopropanol complex. The central mercury ion should be geometrically bound exclusively to the oxygen atoms of the hydroxyl groups, completely bypassing the sulfur atoms.

If the primary product of the high-temperature intramolecular dehydration of ethanol was a saturated ether, draw the resulting gaseous emission permeating an aqueous bromine solution. The liquid medium should permanently retain its low-frequency (reddish-brown) optical state, exhibiting zero electrophilic addition.

category: Characteristic Reaction of Ketones (酮的特性反应)
prompt: If the central carbon atom of the ketone functional group lacked a partial positive electrostatic charge, draw the chemical interaction vessel containing acetone and the hydrazine reagent. The liquid medium should maintain total optical transmittance, exhibiting zero generation of insoluble mid-frequency (yellow) solid mass.

If the electrochemical oxidation potential hierarchy was inverted (Al > Mg > Na), draw three distinct metallic masses side-by-side with explicit nomenclature labels. The mass labeled 'Aluminum' should exhibit severe oxidative structural degradation, while the mass labeled 'Sodium' maintains a pristine, high-reflectance metallic optical state.

If the oxygen atom of the phenolic hydroxyl group lacked available non-bonding electron pairs for coordination, draw the chemical interaction vessel containing phenol and iron(III) chloride. The liquid medium should maintain its initial high-transmittance optical state, exhibiting zero generation of the high-frequency (purple) coordination complex.

If the synthesized silver acetylide compound exhibited high aqueous solubility, draw the chemical interaction vessel where gaseous alkyne permeates the silver ammonia solution. The liquid medium should maintain total optical transmittance, exhibiting zero generation of insoluble multi-spectrum (white) solid mass.

If the aromatic hydrocarbon ring of aniline lacked susceptibility to electrophilic substitution, draw the chemical interaction vessel containing the amine and aqueous bromine. The liquid medium should permanently retain its low-frequency (reddish-brown) optical state, exhibiting zero generation of insoluble multi-spectrum (white) solid mass.
